Name: kv.pcr_ecc_signing_key
Description: Export of ECC signing key words derived from internal KEY_ENTRY storage.

Name: kv.pcr_mldsa_signing_key
Description: Export of MLDSA signing key words derived from internal KEY_ENTRY storage.

Name: kv.key_entry_next
Description: Internal per-entry/per-dword next-data values used to update stored key entries.

Name: kv.lock_use_q
Description: Per-entry use-lock qualified register that prevents reads/uses when set.

Name: kv.uc_req_rdata
Description: Internal read-data returned from register layer to the AHB interface.

Name: kv.hrdata_o
Description: AHB Lite read-data output presented to the bus for master reads.

Name: kv.key_entry_we
Description: Per-entry/per-dword write-enable signals used to gate writes into key storage.

Name: kv.key_entry_ctrl_we
Description: Per-entry control write-enable used for control fields (dest_valid/last_dword).

Name: kv.key_entry_last_dword_next
Description: Per-entry next-value indicating the final dword offset for reads/writes.

Name: kv.uc_req_addr
Description: Internal address presented to the register interface (qualified AHB address).

Name: kv.haddr_i
Description: AHB Lite address bus from the master to this slave.

Name: kv.hwdata_i
Description: AHB Lite write-data bus used to write register or key data.

Name: kv.hwrite_i
Description: AHB Lite write control that indicates a write transaction.

Name: kv.hsel_i
Description: AHB Lite slave select signal that enables this module for a transaction.

Name: kv.cptra_in_debug_scan_mode
Description: Signal indicating the chiplet is in debug/scan mode that affects key flush behavior.

Name: kv.debugUnlock_or_scan_mode_switch
Description: Immediate control signal that forces keyvault flush behavior for debug/unlock transitions.

Name: kv.lock_wr_q
Description: Per-entry write-lock qualified register that prevents further writes when set.

Name: kv.key_entry_clear
Description: Per-entry clear signal that asynchronously clears or marks key entry for clearing.

Name: kv.cptra_pwrgood
Description: Power-good input used to drive hardware qualified reset for register interface.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.zeroize
Description: Control input that forces the FSM and counters to reset/clear state.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.start
Description: Control input to begin the FSM operation from IDLE.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.allow
Description: Control input that gates whether start goes to RW (process) or DONE.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.last
Description: Input flag indicating the last data dword of valid input.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.pcr_hash_extend
Description: Control input that selects a fixed total dword count (pcr extend mode).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.num_dwords
Description: Input specifying the (configured) number of dwords to process when PAD==0.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.write_en
Description: Output write enable that controls external memory writes.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.write_offset
Description: Output offset/address used by external logic for write operations.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.write_pad
Description: Output flag indicating that pad_data should be written instead of input data.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.pad_data
Description: 32-bit output word used for padding writes (either 0x80000000, zeros, or length_for_pad).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.num_dwords_data
Description: Internal register capturing the number of valid data dwords (used for padding length calculation).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.num_dwords_total
Description: Combinational internal total dword count used for termination and offset comparisons.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.length_for_pad
Description: Combinational value computed as the bit-length (in 32-bit words shifted) used as the last pad word (HMAC aware).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.kv_fsm_ps
Description: FSM present state register representing the current state (IDLE, RW, PAD, ZERO, LENGTH, DONE).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.kv_fsm_ns
Description: Combinational next-state signal that drives kv_fsm_ps on clock.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_IDLE_KV_RW
Description: Internal combinational condition (start && allow) used to transition IDLE -> RW.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_IDLE_KV_DONE
Description: Internal combinational condition (start && !allow) used to transition IDLE -> DONE.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_RW_KV_DONE
Description: Internal condition that detects completion of RW stage (either full count reached or last) and routes to DONE.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_DONE_KV_IDLE
Description: Internal combinational always-true condition used to transition DONE -> IDLE.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_RW_KV_PAD
Description: Internal condition indicating transition from RW to PAD when last is seen (PAD mode enabled).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_PAD_KV_ZERO
Description: Internal condition used to move from the PAD state to ZERO state during padding.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_ZERO_KV_LENGTH
Description: Internal condition that decides when to append the length word (ZERO -> LENGTH).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.arc_KV_LENGTH_KV_DONE
Description: Internal condition to transition from LENGTH state to DONE after writing the length word.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.offset_en
Description: Internal control that enables incrementing the offset counter.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.offset_rst
Description: Internal control that resets the offset when a done condition or length-done occurs.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.offset
Description: Register holding the current offset (used to form read_offset and write_offset).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.offset_nxt
Description: Combinational next value for the offset register (offset + 1 typically).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.read_offset
Description: Output address (offset) used by external logic to read input data while in RW.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.write_last
Description: Output flag indicating that the current write is the final write of the sequence.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.ready
Description: Output that indicates the FSM is in IDLE and ready to accept a start.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_fsm.done
Description: Output indicating the FSM has finished its processing and is in DONE state.

Name: kv_read_client.zeroize
Description: Input control that triggers zeroization handling within the client.

Name: kv_read_client.write_en
Description: Output write enable asserted when the client issues a write beat to downstream storage/interface.

Name: kv_read_client.write_offset
Description: Output offset/address for each write beat produced by the client.

Name: kv_read_client.error_code
Description: Status output encoding whether the last read produced an error condition or not.

Name: kv_read_client.validated_read_en
Description: Internal control (delayed read enable) used to start the read client FSM after rules check.

Name: kv_read_client.read_allow
Description: Internal allow/permission signal produced by the rule-check block to permit the read FSM.

Name: kv_read_client.read_offset
Description: Internal read-side offset/address value used to form the kv_read.read_offset output.

Name: kv_read_client.write_pad
Description: Internal flag selecting whether pad_data or the forwarded read data is presented on write_data.

Name: kv_read_client.pad_data
Description: Internal 32-bit value used as the pad content when write_pad is asserted.

Name: kv_read_client.write_data
Description: Output data beat produced by the client (either padded data or forwarded KV read data).

Name: kv_read_client.kv_ready
Description: Output ready signal indicating the read client FSM is ready to proceed/accept operations.

Name: kv_read_client.read_done
Description: Output indicating completion of the read operation.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_rules.read_en_i
Description: Input enable that starts the rule-check and, when asserted, causes the module to evaluate policy and drive read_en_o.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_rules.read_allow
Description: Output policy result indicating whether the requested read is permitted by the module's rules.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_rules.rule_fail.no_read_key_release
Description: Internal flag that indicates a rule failure condition preventing reads from the key-release slot when OCP lock is in progress.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_rules.read_done
Description: Input indication that a read operation is complete and that the module should clear its outputs/state for the next transaction.

Name: kv_read_client.kv_read_rules.read_en_o
Description: Output enable that starts the downstream read client FSM when the rule-check allows the read.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.s_cpuif_req
Description: CPU-side request input signal that initiates register read/write transactions.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.s_cpuif_req_is_wr
Description: CPU-side indicator that distinguishes write from read transactions.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.s_cpuif_addr
Description: CPU-side address bus selecting which register is accessed.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.s_cpuif_wr_data
Description: CPU-side write data bus carrying data to be written to registers.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.s_cpuif_wr_biten
Description: CPU-side write byte-enable mask controlling which bytes of a write are committed.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.cpuif_req
Description: Internal copy of the CPU request handshake (mirrors s_cpuif_req).

Name: kv.kv_reg1.cpuif_req_is_wr
Description: Internal copy of read/write indicator for requests.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.cpuif_addr
Description: Internal address bus used for register decode.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.cpuif_wr_data
Description: Internal write-data signal forwarded to the decode/field logic.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.cpuif_wr_biten
Description: Internal forwarded write byte-enable mask.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.decoded_reg_strb
Description: Decoded per-register strobes that indicate which register is being accessed.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.decoded_req
Description: Forwarded internal request signal used by the decoded stage.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.decoded_req_is_wr
Description: Forwarded write/read indicator used by decode/field logic.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.decoded_wr_data
Description: Internal copy of the write data presented to field update logic.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.decoded_wr_biten
Description: Internal copy of write byte-enable mask used during field updates.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.KEY_ENTRY[].data.value
Description: Stored 32-bit values for KEY_ENTRY registers (per-key storage words).

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.KEY_CTRL[].lock_wr.value
Description: Per-key control bit that locks write updates for that KEY_ENTRY.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.KEY_CTRL[].lock_use.value
Description: Per-key control bit that locks usage of a key (prevents key usage changes).

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.KEY_CTRL[].dest_valid.value
Description: Per-key destination-valid vector indicating valid destinations for this key.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.KEY_CTRL[].last_dword.value
Description: Per-key field indicating the index of the last valid data word for a key entry.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.KEY_CTRL[].clear.value
Description: Per-key single-pulse-clear indicator exposed in KEY_CTRL.

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.CLEAR_SECRETS.wr_debug_values.value
Description: Control bit used to write/trigger clearing of debug values (single-pulse behavior).

Name: kv.kv_reg1.field_storage.CLEAR_SECRETS.sel_debug_value.value
Description: Control bit that selects which debug value is exposed.

Name: kv_write_client.zeroize
Description: Control input that requests clearing/zeroizing operational state.

Name: kv_write_client.num_dwords
Description: Input specifying the number of data double-words to write.

Name: kv_write_client.dest_keyvault
Description: Output indicating whether the destination is the keyvault.

Name: kv_write_client.dest_data_avail
Description: Input handshake signal that indicates client data is available to be consumed.

Name: kv_write_client.kv_ready
Description: Output ready handshake from the write FSM indicating the client is ready.

Name: kv_write_client.dest_done
Description: Output that signals completion of the destination write operation.

Name: kv_write_client.error_code
Description: Output status code that indicates error or success of write operations.

Name: kv_write_client.write_allow
Description: Internal signal produced by rule-checking logic that enables/blocks writes.

Name: kv_write_client.dest_read_offset
Description: Internal read index used to select which word from dest_data to send to the writer.

Name: kv_write_client.dest_write_offset
Description: Internal write-offset/address used to compute the write destination within the key-value store.

Name: kv_write_client.dest_write_en
Description: Internal enable signal that triggers actual write beats to the kv interface.

Name: kv_write_client.write_pad
Description: Internal flag indicating whether the current beat is a padding beat.

Name: kv_write_client.write_last
Description: Internal signal marking the last beat of a multi-beat write.

Name: kv_write_client.kv_write_rules.write_allow
Description: Registered output that indicates whether a KV write operation is permitted.

Name: kv_write_client.kv_write_rules.rule_fail.aes_only_to_key_release
Description: Internal rule flag that indicates a failure when non‑AES sources attempt to write the key‑release slot during OCP lock.

Name: kv_write_client.kv_write_rules.rule_fail.std_to_std
Description: Internal rule flag that signals failure when an input from the standard region is not written to a standard destination during OCP lock.

Name: kv_write_client.kv_write_rules.rule_fail.lock_to_lock
Description: Internal rule flag that signals failure when input from the OCP lock region is not written to a lock destination during OCP lock.

Name: kv_write_client.kv_write_rules.rule_fail.aes_dec_to_rt_obf_key
Description: Internal rule flag that verifies AES ECB decrypt with a specific RT obfuscated key results in the correct destination (key‑release slot) under OCP lock.
